query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 12b6eda3).

## Issue to Resolve

A type with multiple annotations inside a TypeArgumentList is not formatted correctly
Code below:
```
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>,
)
```

is formatted as:
```
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull
    FooDto,>,
)
```

KtLint: 0.47.1

_Originally posted by @Vadgyik in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/673#issuecomment-1351480443_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

When I really exaggerate the example code, it turns out that it is not so easy to find an acceptable outcome that is deterministic when formatting generic type parameters that are annotated.

Suppose I have following classes and annotations:
```
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class Foo
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class Bar1
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class Bar2
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class FooBarLongDescription
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class ComplexAnnotationWithParameters(val string: String)
class SimpleDto
class LongerButReasonableDto
class ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto
class SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<A, B, C>
```

Example A below shows the code in which annotations are not wrapped:
```
data class ExampleA(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList: List<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto>,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap: Map<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto, @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto>,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType: SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto, @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto, @Suppress("AnnotationRule") @Foo @FooBarLongDescription ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto>,
)
```

This can be made more readable by wrapping each generic type having multiple annotations:
```
data class ExampleB(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList:
        List<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto,
        >,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap: Map<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto,
        >,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType:
        SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto,
            @ComplexAnnotationWithParameters("foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar") @Foo @FooBarLongDescription ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto,
        >,
)
```
A valid question with the example above is whether the same should also have be done with `atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>`. The biggest downside of this approach is that formatting is not consistent with the way that annotations are formatted on normal parameters or classes.

If each annotation is wrapped to a separate line, the result would look like:
```
data class ExampleC(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList: List<
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        @Bar1
        @Bar2
        SimpleDto
    >,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap: Map<
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        @Bar1
        @Bar2
        SimpleDto,
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        LongerButReasonableDto
    >,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType: SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        @Bar1
        @Bar2
        SimpleDto,
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        LongerButReasonableDto,
        @ComplexAnnotationWithParameters("foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar")
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto,
    >,
)
```
Advantage is that this is consistent with other formatting. Disadvantage of course is that it is lengthy. Despite that, I am inclined to choose this formatting.

But when combined with the rule suppression mechanism, the developer could choose in specific case to use a manual formatting like below:
```
@Suppress("ktlint:annotation")
data class ExampleD(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList: List<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription SimpleDto>,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap:
        Map<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription
            SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription
            LongerButReasonableDto
        >,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType:
        SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription
            LongerButReasonableDto,
            @ComplexAnnotationWithParameters("foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar")
            @Foo
            @FooBarLongDescription
            ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto,
        >,
)
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/faq.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ASTNodeExtension.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/AnnotationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ArgumentListWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BlockCommentInitialStarAlignmentRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ChainWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/CommentWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ContextReceiverWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionSignatureRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IfElseBracingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/KdocWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ModifierListSpacingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultiLineIfElseRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoEmptyFirstLineInClassBodyRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/WrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this issue. The problem is that when there are multiple annotations inside a TypeArgumentList (like `List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>`), the formatting is incorrect. The code is being formatted as:

```
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull
    FooDto,>,
)
```

instead of staying on the same line or being properly formatted.

Let me start by understanding the codebase structure and the relevant files mentioned.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION

  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ANNOTATION_ENTRY
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOf
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 30→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.descriptors.annotations.AnnotationUseSiteTarget
 31→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 32→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 33→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 34→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.safeAs
 35→
 36→/**
 37→ * Ensures multiple annotations are not on the same line as the annotated declaration. Also ensures that annotations
 38→ * with parameters are placed on separate lines.
 39→ *
 40→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 41→ *
 42→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules. Moved since
 43→ */
 44→public class AnnotationRule : StandardRule("annotation") {
 45→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 46→        node: ASTNode,
 47→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 49→    ) {
 50→        when (node.elementType) {
 51→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
 52→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 53→            }
 54→            ANNOTATION -> {
 55→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 56→            }
 57→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY ->
 58→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 59→        }
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
 63→        node: ASTNode,
 64→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 65→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 66→    ) {
 67→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
 68→
 69→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 70→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 71→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")
 72→            !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
 73→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
 74→        ) {
 75→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
 79→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
 80→        ) {
 81→            checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 82→        }
 83→
 84→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
 85→            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
 86→            node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
 87→        ) {
 88→            checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→        }
 90→    }
 91→
 92→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 93→        node: ASTNode,
 94→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 95→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 96→    ) {
 97→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                node.startOffset,
100→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                node
105→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
106→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
107→            }
108→        }
109→
110→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
111→            emit(
112→                node.startOffset,
113→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
114→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
115→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
116→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
117→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
118→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
119→            )
120→            if (autoCorrect) {
121→                node
122→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
123→                    .nextLeaf()
124→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
125→                    ?.let {
126→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
127→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
128→                        } else {
129→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
130→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
131→                            )
132→                        }
133→                    }
134→            }
135→        }
136→    }
137→
138→    private fun checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(
139→        node: ASTNode,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
142→    ) {
143→        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
144→            val noAnnotationWithParameters =
145→                node
146→                    .siblings(forward = false)
147→                    .none { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
148→            if (noAnnotationWithParameters) {
149→                emit(
150→                    node.treeParent.startOffset,
151→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
152→                    true,
153→                )
154→                if (autoCorrect) {
155→                    node
156→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
157→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
158→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
159→                }
160→            }
161→        }
162→    }
163→
164→    private fun checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
165→        node: ASTNode,
166→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168→    ) {
169→        val isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList =
170→            node
171→                .siblings(forward = true)
172→                .any { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
173→        val isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine =
174→            node
175→                .siblings(forward = false)
176→                .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
177→        if (isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList || isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine) {
178→            emit(
179→                node.startOffset,
180→                "Annotation must be placed on separate line",
181→                true,
182→            )
183→            if (autoCorrect) {
184→                node
185→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
186→                    .nextLeaf()
187→                    ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→
192→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
193→
194→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
195→        psi
196→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
197→            ?.useSiteTarget
198→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
199→
200→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
201→
202→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
203→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
204→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
205→
206→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
207→        treeParent
208→            .children()
209→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
210→            .let { it == this }
211→
212→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = false).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
213→
214→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
215→        siblings(forward = false)
216→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
217→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
218→
219→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
220→
221→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
222→        siblings(forward = true)
223→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
224→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
225→
226→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() =
227→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
228→            .leaves(forward = true)
229→            .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() }
230→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
231→
232→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
233→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
234→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
235→        } else {
236→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
237→        }
238→
239→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
240→        node: ASTNode,
241→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
242→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
243→    ) {
244→        node
245→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
246→            .nextCodeLeaf()
247→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
248→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
249→
250→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
251→                    emit(
252→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
253→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
254→                        true,
255→                    )
256→                    if (autoCorrect) {
257→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
258→                    }
259→                }
260→            }
261→    }
262→
263→    private fun visitAnnotation(
264→        node: ASTNode,
265→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
267→    ) {
268→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
269→
270→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
271→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
272→        ) {
273→            emit(
274→                node.startOffset,
275→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
276→                true,
277→            )
278→            if (autoCorrect) {
279→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
280→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
281→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
282→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
283→                }
284→            }
285→        }
286→    }
287→
288→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
289→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
290→        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first
291→        // text in the file
292→        val newLineWithIndent = nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text ?: "\n"
293→        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
294→            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
295→            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
296→        } else {
297→            newLineWithIndent
298→        }
299→    }
300→}
301→
302→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
303→

 71→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtSuperTypeList

 74→
 75→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 76→
 77→/**
 78→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 79→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 80→ *
 81→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 82→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 83→ * indentation rule is not run.
 84→ */
 85→public class WrappingRule :
 86→    StandardRule(
 87→        id = "wrapping",
 88→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 89→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 90→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 91→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 92→        ),
 93→    ) {
 94→    private var line = 1
 95→    private lateinit var indentConfig: IndentConfig
 96→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        line = 1
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→    ) {
112→        when (node.elementType) {
113→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
114→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
115→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
116→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
119→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
129→
130→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
131→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
132→            return
133→        }
134→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
135→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
136→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
137→        val wrapBlock =
138→            when {
139→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
140→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
141→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
142→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
143→                }
144→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
145→                    val startOfLine = node.prevLeaf {
146→                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
147→                    }
148→                    val endOfLine = node.nextLeaf {
149→                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
150→                    }
151→                    val line =
152→                        startOfLine
153→                            ?.leaves()
154→                            ?.takeWhile { it != endOfLine }
155→                            ?.joinToString(separator = "", prefix = startOfLine.text.removePrefix("\n")) { it.text }
156→                            .orEmpty()
157→                    line.length > maxLineLength
158→                }
159→                else -> false
160→            }
161→        if (wrapBlock) {
162→            startOfBlock
163→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
164→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
165→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
166→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
167→                        autoCorrect,
168→                        emit,
169→                    )
170→                }
171→        }
172→    }
173→
174→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
175→        node: ASTNode,
176→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
177→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
178→    ) {
179→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
180→        var newlineInBetween = false
181→        var parameterListInBetween = false
182→        var numberOfArgs = 0
183→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
184→        // matching ), ] or }
185→        val r = node.nextSibling {
186→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
187→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
188→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
189→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
190→            if (isValueArgument) {
191→                numberOfArgs++
192→                firstArg = it
193→            }
194→            it.elementType == rElementType
195→        }!!
196→        if (
197→            !newlineInBetween ||
198→            // keep { p ->
199→            // }
200→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
201→            // keep ({
202→            // }) and (object : C {
203→            // })
204→            (
205→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
206→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
207→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
208→                )
209→        ) {
210→            return
211→        }
212→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
213→            // keep:
214→            // for (foo in listOf(
215→            //     "foo-1",
216→            //     "foo-2"
217→            // )) { ... }
218→            // but reject:
219→            // for (
220→            //     foo in listOf(
221→            //         "foo-1",
222→            //         "foo-2"
223→            //     )
224→            // ) { ... }
225→            return
226→        }
227→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
228→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
229→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
230→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
231→                    it.textLength > 0
232→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
233→            // IDEA quirk:
234→            // if (true &&
235→            //     true
236→            // ) {
237→            // }
238→            // instead of
239→            // if (
240→            //     true &&
241→            //     true
242→            // ) {
243→            // }
244→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
245→        ) {
246→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
247→        }
248→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
249→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.lineIndent())
250→        }
251→    }
252→
253→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
254→        node: ASTNode,
255→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
256→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
257→    ) {
258→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
259→        if (
260→            node.textContains('\n') &&
261→            entries.size > 1 &&
262→            // e.g.
263→            //
264→            // class A : B, C,
265→            //     D
266→            // or
267→            // class A : B, C({
268→            // }), D
269→            //
270→            // but not
271→            //
272→            // class A : B, C, D({
273→            // })
274→            !(
275→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
276→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
277→                )
278→        ) {
279→            // put space after :
280→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
281→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
282→                if (
283→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
284→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
285→                ) {
286→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
287→                }
288→            }
289→            // put entries on separate lines
290→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
291→            for (c in node.children()) {
292→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
293→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
294→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
295→                ) {
296→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
297→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
298→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
299→                        emit = emit,
300→                        indent = node.lineIndent(),
301→                    )
302→                }
303→            }
304→        }
305→    }
306→
307→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
308→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
309→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
310→
311→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
312→        node: ASTNode,
313→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
314→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
315→    ) {
316→        for (c in node.children()) {
317→            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
318→                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
319→                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
320→                else -> false
321→            }
322→            if (hasLineBreak) {
323→                // rearrange
324→                //
325→                // a, b, value(
326→                // ), c, d
327→                //
328→                // to
329→                //
330→                // a, b,
331→                // value(
332→                // ),
333→                // c, d
334→
335→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
336→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
337→                if (
338→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
339→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340→                ) {
341→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
342→                }
343→                // insert \n after multi-line value
344→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
345→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
346→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
347→                if (
348→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
349→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
350→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
351→                    // value(
352→                    // ), // a comment
353→                    // c, d
354→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
355→                ) {
356→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
357→                }
358→            }
359→        }
360→    }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
363→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
364→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
365→
366→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
367→        node: ASTNode,
368→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
369→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
370→    ) {
371→        node
372→            .treeParent
373→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
374→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
375→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
376→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
377→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
378→            ?.let {
379→                // rewriting
380→                // """
381→                //     text
382→                // _""".trimIndent()
383→                // to
384→                // """
385→                //     text
386→                // _
387→                // """.trimIndent()
388→                emit(
389→                    node.startOffset,
390→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
391→                    true,
392→                )
393→                if (autoCorrect) {
394→                    node as LeafPsiElement
395→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
396→                }
397→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
398→            }
399→    }
400→
401→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
402→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
403→        // if token is in lTokenSet
404→        //     find matching rToken
405→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
406→        // return false
407→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
408→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
409→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
410→            // special cases:
411→            // x = y.f({ z ->
412→            // })
413→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
414→            // 2, 3)
415→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
416→        }
417→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
418→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
419→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
420→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
421→        }
422→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
423→            return true
424→        }
425→        return false
426→    }
427→
428→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
429→        node: ASTNode,
430→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
431→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
432→    ) {
433→        val p = node.treeParent
434→        if (
435→            // check
436→            // `{ p -> ... }`
437→            // and
438→            // `when { m -> ... }`
439→            // only
440→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
441→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
442→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
443→            // permit
444→            // when {
445→            //     m -> 0 + d({
446→            //     })
447→            // }
448→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
449→            // permit
450→            // when (this) {
451→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
452→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
453→            //     -> true
454→            // }
455→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
456→        ) {
457→            return
458→        }
459→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
460→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
461→        }
462→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
463→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
464→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
465→        }
466→    }
467→
468→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
469→        node: ASTNode,
470→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
471→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
472→        indent: String,
473→    ) {
474→        emit(
475→            node.startOffset - 1,
476→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
477→            true,
478→        )
479→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
480→        if (autoCorrect) {
481→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
482→        }
483→    }
484→
485→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
486→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
487→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
488→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
489→        indent: String? = null,
490→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
491→    ) {
492→        emit(
493→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
494→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
495→            true,
496→        )
497→        LOGGER.trace {
498→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
499→        }
500→        if (autoCorrect) {
501→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
502→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + tempIndent)
503→        }
504→    }
505→
506→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
507→        for (child in node.children()) {
508→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
509→                val v = child.text
510→                if (v == "\n") {
511→                    return true
512→                }
513→            }
514→        }
515→        return false
516→    }
517→
518→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
519→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
520→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
521→            textContains('\n')
522→        } else {
523→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
524→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
525→        }
526→    }
527→
528→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
529→
530→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
531→
532→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
533→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
534→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
535→
536→    /**
537→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
538→     *     for (foo in listOf(
539→     *         "foo-1",
540→     *         "foo-2"
541→     *     )) { ... }
542→     * but reject:
543→     *     for (
544→     *         foo in listOf(
545→     *             "foo-1",
546→     *             "foo-2"
547→     *         )
548→     *     ) { ... }
549→     */
550→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
551→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
552→            return false
553→        }
554→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
555→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
556→        }
557→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
558→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
559→        }
560→
561→        var node: ASTNode? = this
562→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
563→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
564→                return false
565→            }
566→            node = node.nextSibling { true }
567→        }
568→        return true
569→    }
570→
571→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
572→        node: ASTNode,
573→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
574→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
575→    ) {
576→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
577→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
578→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
579→                return
580→            }
581→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
582→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
583→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
584→            val wrapBlock =
585→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
586→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
587→                    )
588→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
589→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
590→                    endOfBlock,
591→                    autoCorrect,
592→                    emit,
593→                    node.treeParent.lineIndent(),
594→                )
595→            }
596→        }
597→    }
598→
599→    private companion object {
600→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
601→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
602→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
603→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
604→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
605→            ).toMap()
606→    }
607→}
608→
609→public val WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = WrappingRule().ruleId

```kotlin
!node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
```

This suggests that the annotation rule is supposed to skip annotations inside TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST. Let me look at more files to understand the issue better.

content.thinking:
Now I see in the test at lines 329-344, there's a test `Issue 642 - Given annotations on type arguments on same line as argument` that shows that annotations in TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST should stay on the same line. The test shows code like `Map<@Ann("test") Int, @JvmSuppressWildcards(true) (String) -> Int?>` should not have linting violations.

But the issue reported is that when there are multiple annotations like `List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>`, it's being incorrectly formatted as:
```
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull
    FooDto,>,
)
```

Let me look at the AnnotationRule more carefully. I see that for annotations in TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST:
1. Line 72: `!node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST)` - This skips annotations with parameters that are inside TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST
2. But the other checks (lines 78-89) don't seem to skip TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST

Let me look at what happens when there are multiple annotations. The issue might be in the `checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct` function or `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine` function.

Looking at lines 78-89:
```kotlin
if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
    (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
) {
    checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
}

if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
    node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
    node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
) {
    checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
}
```

I'll need to modify the condition to explicitly exclude TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST when checking multiple annotations. The current implementation doesn't account for type argument annotations, which causes the incorrect formatting.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue better. The problem is that the `AnnotationRule` doesn't properly check for TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST in some of its conditions.

Looking at the AnnotationRule's `visitAnnotationEntry` function:

1. For line 72, annotations with parameters that are inside TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST are correctly skipped.
2. However, at lines 78-89, the conditions for multiple annotations don't have a check for TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST.

Let me trace through what happens with `List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>`:
- When processing `@Valid`:
  - Line 78-81: `node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()` returns true (because `@NotNull` follows), and `node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()` returns true. So `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine` is called.
  - Line 84-89: This might also trigger a check.

So the issue is that lines 78-89 are missing the TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST check. Let me verify this is the root cause.

Looking at the test case on lines 329-344:
```kotlin
@Test
fun `Issue 642 - Given annotations on type arguments on same line as argument`() {
    val code =
        """
        val aProperty: Map<@Ann("test") Int, @JvmSuppressWildcards(true) (String) -> Int?>
        val bProperty: Map<
            @Ann String,
            @Ann("test") Int,
            @JvmSuppressWildcards(true) (String) -> Int?
            >

        fun doSomething() {
            funWithGenericsCall<@JvmSuppressWildcards(true) Int>()
        }
        """.trimIndent()
    annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()


}
```

I'll need to modify the annotation rule to handle multiple annotations within TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST correctly. The current implementation doesn't account for annotations in generic type arguments when multiple annotations are present.

The solution involves adding a specific check for TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST context when processing multiple annotations on the same line. This will prevent unnecessary linting violations for type argument annotations.

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. Looking at the `AnnotationRule`, I can see that:

1. At line 72, annotations with parameters that are inside TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST are correctly skipped.
2. However, at lines 78-89, the conditions for multiple annotations don't have a check for TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST.

This means when there are multiple annotations like `List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>`, the annotation rule incorrectly tries to put them on separate lines.

Let me write a quick test to verify the problem:

content.thinking:
Now I can see the specific issue. The error is:
```
LintError(line=2, col=20, ruleId=standard:annotation, detail=Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct, canBeAutoCorrected=true)
```

This confirms my analysis. The issue is in the `checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct` function which doesn't check for TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST.

Looking at the AnnotationRule code again:
```kotlin
if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
    node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
    node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
) {
    checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
}
```

I need to add a check for TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST to skip annotations inside type arguments.

Also looking at:
```kotlin
if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
    (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
) {
    checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
}
```

This also might need to be guarded with a TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST check.

Let me fix the AnnotationRule.

content.text:
I've confirmed the issue. The error message is:
```
Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct
```

This confirms that annotations inside TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST are not being properly skipped. Let me fix the `AnnotationRule`:

 36→/**
 37→ * Ensures multiple annotations are not on the same line as the annotated declaration. Also ensures that annotations
 38→ * with parameters are placed on separate lines.
 39→ *
 40→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 41→ *
 42→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules. Moved since
 43→ */
 44→public class AnnotationRule : StandardRule("annotation") {
 45→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 46→        node: ASTNode,
 47→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 49→    ) {
 50→        when (node.elementType) {
 51→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
 52→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 53→            }
 54→            ANNOTATION -> {
 55→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 56→            }
 57→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY ->
 58→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 59→        }
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
 63→        node: ASTNode,
 64→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 65→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 66→    ) {
 67→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
 68→
 69→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 70→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 71→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")
 72→            !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
 73→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
 74→        ) {
 75→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
 79→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
 80→        ) {
 81→            checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 82→        }
 83→
 84→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
 85→            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
 86→            node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
 87→        ) {
 88→            checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→        }
 90→    }
 91→
 92→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 93→        node: ASTNode,
 94→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 95→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 96→    ) {
 97→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                node.startOffset,
100→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                node
105→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
106→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
107→            }
108→        }
109→
110→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
111→            emit(
112→                node.startOffset,
113→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
114→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
115→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
116→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
117→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
118→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
119→            )
120→            if (autoCorrect) {
121→                node
122→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
123→                    .nextLeaf()
124→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
125→                    ?.let {
126→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
127→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
128→                        } else {
129→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
130→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
131→                            )
132→                        }
133→                    }
134→            }
135→        }
136→    }
137→
138→    private fun checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(
139→        node: ASTNode,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
142→    ) {
143→        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
144→            val noAnnotationWithParameters =
145→                node
146→                    .siblings(forward = false)
147→                    .none { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
148→            if (noAnnotationWithParameters) {
149→                emit(
150→                    node.treeParent.startOffset,
151→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
152→                    true,
153→                )
154→                if (autoCorrect) {
155→                    node
156→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
157→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
158→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
159→                }
160→            }
161→        }
162→    }
163→
164→    private fun checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
165→        node: ASTNode,
166→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168→    ) {
169→        val isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList =
170→            node
171→                .siblings(forward = true)
172→                .any { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
173→        val isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine =
174→            node
175→                .siblings(forward = false)
176→                .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
177→        if (isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList || isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine) {
178→            emit(
179→                node.startOffset,
180→                "Annotation must be placed on separate line",
181→                true,
182→            )
183→            if (autoCorrect) {
184→                node
185→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
186→                    .nextLeaf()
187→                    ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→
192→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
193→
194→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
195→        psi
196→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
197→            ?.useSiteTarget
198→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
199→
200→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
201→
202→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
203→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
204→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
205→
206→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
207→        treeParent
208→            .children()
209→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
210→            .let { it == this }
211→
212→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = false).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
213→
214→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
215→        siblings(forward = false)
216→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
217→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
218→
219→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
220→
221→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
222→        siblings(forward = true)
223→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
224→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
225→
226→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() =
227→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
228→            .leaves(forward = true)
229→            .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() }
230→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
231→
232→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
233→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
234→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
235→        } else {
236→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
237→        }
238→
239→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
240→        node: ASTNode,
241→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
242→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
243→    ) {
244→        node
245→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
246→            .nextCodeLeaf()
247→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
248→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
249→
250→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
251→                    emit(
252→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
253→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
254→                        true,
255→                    )
256→                    if (autoCorrect) {
257→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
258→                    }
259→                }
260→            }
261→    }
262→
263→    private fun visitAnnotation(
264→        node: ASTNode,
265→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
267→    ) {
268→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
269→
270→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
271→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
272→        ) {

288→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
289→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
290→        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first
291→        // text in the file
292→        val newLineWithIndent = nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text ?: "\n"
293→        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
294→            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
295→            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
296→        } else {
297→            newLineWithIndent
298→        }
299→    }
300→}

content.thinking:
All tests pass. Let me summarize what I've done:

1. **Analyzed the issue**: The problem was that when there are multiple annotations inside a TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST (like `List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>`), the AnnotationRule was incorrectly treating them like regular annotations and trying to place them on separate lines.

2. **Identified the root cause**: In the `AnnotationRule.kt`, there were already checks for TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST at line 72 (for annotations with parameters), but the checks at lines 78-89 (for multiple annotations on the same line as annotated construct, and annotations that should be on separate lines) were missing the TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST check.

3. **Fixed the issue**: Added `!node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST)` checks to both conditions at lines 78-89 to skip annotations that are inside a TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST.

4. **Verified the fix**: Created new test cases that reproduce the issue and verified they pass with the fix. Also ran all existing tests to ensure no regressions.

The fix is minimal and targeted, addressing only the specific issue while maintaining all existing functionality.

content.thinking:
The fix is in place. Let me provide a summary of what was done:

## Summary

The issue was that multiple annotations inside a `TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST` (like `List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>`) were incorrectly being reported as violations and incorrectly formatted.

### Root Cause
In `AnnotationRule.kt`, there was already a check for `TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST` at line 72 (for annotations with parameters), but the additional checks at lines 78-89 were missing this guard:

1. **Line 78-82**: The check for forcing annotations onto separate lines when followed/preceded by other annotations
2. **Line 85-90**: The check for multiple annotations on the same line as the annotated construct

### Fix
Added `!node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST)` to both conditions to skip annotations that are inside a TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST. This ensures that annotations inside generic type arguments (like `Map<@Foo @Bar String, Int>`) are not subject to the normal annotation formatting rules.

### Tests
Added a new test file `AnnotationRuleTypeArgumentTest.kt` with 7 test cases covering:
- Simple type with multiple annotations in TypeArgumentList
- Annotations with parameters in TypeArgumentList
- Map with multiple annotated types
- Generics with many type arguments
- Complex annotation parameters
- Function calls with annotated type arguments
- Annotations on separate lines within TypeArgumentList

All existing tests continue to pass, confirming no regression.

content.text:
The fix is complete. Let me provide a summary:

## Summary

**Issue**: A type with multiple annotations inside a TypeArgumentList was being incorrectly formatted. Code like:
```kotlin
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>,
)
```
was being formatted incorrectly as:
```kotlin
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull
    FooDto,>,
)
```

**Root Cause**: In `AnnotationRule.kt`, there was already a check for `TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST` at line 72 (for annotations with parameters), but two additional checks (lines 78-89) were missing this guard:
1. The check for forcing annotations onto separate lines when followed/preceded by other annotations
2. The check for multiple annotations on the same line as the annotated construct

**Fix**: Added `!node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST)` to both conditions in the `visitAnnotationEntry` function. This ensures that annotations inside generic type arguments are not subject to the normal annotation formatting rules that would otherwise force them onto separate lines.

**Files Changed**:
1. `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/AnnotationRule.kt` - Added TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST checks to lines 78 and 85

**Files Added**:
1. `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/AnnotationRuleTypeArgumentTest.kt` - New test file with 7 test cases covering various scenarios

All tests pass, including the new tests and all existing tests, confirming the fix works correctly without any regressions.

 36→/**
 37→ * Ensures multiple annotations are not on the same line as the annotated declaration. Also ensures that annotations
 38→ * with parameters are placed on separate lines.
 39→ *
 40→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 41→ *
 42→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules. Moved since
 43→ */
 44→public class AnnotationRule : StandardRule("annotation") {
 45→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 46→        node: ASTNode,
 47→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 49→    ) {
 50→        when (node.elementType) {
 51→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
 52→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 53→            }
 54→            ANNOTATION -> {
 55→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 56→            }
 57→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY ->
 58→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 59→        }
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
 63→        node: ASTNode,
 64→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 65→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 66→    ) {
 67→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
 68→
 69→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 70→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 71→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")
 72→            !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
 73→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
 74→        ) {
 75→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
 79→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
 80→        ) {
 81→            checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 82→        }
 83→
 84→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
 85→            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
 86→            node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
 87→        ) {
 88→            checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→        }
 90→    }
 91→
 92→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 93→        node: ASTNode,
 94→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 95→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 96→    ) {
 97→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                node.startOffset,
100→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                node
105→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
106→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
107→            }
108→        }
109→
110→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
111→            emit(
112→                node.startOffset,
113→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
114→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
115→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
116→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
117→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
118→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
119→            )
120→            if (autoCorrect) {
121→                node
122→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
123→                    .nextLeaf()
124→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
125→                    ?.let {
126→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
127→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
128→                        } else {
129→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
130→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
131→                            )
132→                        }
133→                    }
134→            }
135→        }
136→    }
137→
138→    private fun checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(
139→        node: ASTNode,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
142→    ) {
143→        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
144→            val noAnnotationWithParameters =
145→                node
146→                    .siblings(forward = false)
147→                    .none { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
148→            if (noAnnotationWithParameters) {
149→                emit(
150→                    node.treeParent.startOffset,
151→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
152→                    true,
153→                )
154→                if (autoCorrect) {
155→                    node
156→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
157→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
158→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
159→                }
160→            }
161→        }
162→    }
163→
164→    private fun checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
165→        node: ASTNode,
166→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168→    ) {
169→        val isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList =
170→            node
171→                .siblings(forward = true)
172→                .any { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
173→        val isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine =
174→            node
175→                .siblings(forward = false)
176→                .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
177→        if (isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList || isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine) {
178→            emit(
179→                node.startOffset,
180→                "Annotation must be placed on separate line",
181→                true,
182→            )
183→            if (autoCorrect) {
184→                node
185→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
186→                    .nextLeaf()
187→                    ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→
192→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
193→
194→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
195→        psi
196→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
197→            ?.useSiteTarget
198→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
199→
200→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
201→
202→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
203→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
204→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
205→
206→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
207→        treeParent
208→            .children()
209→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
210→            .let { it == this }
211→
212→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = false).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
213→
214→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
215→        siblings(forward = false)
216→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
217→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
218→
219→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
220→
221→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
222→        siblings(forward = true)
223→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
224→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
225→
226→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() =
227→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
228→            .leaves(forward = true)
229→            .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() }
230→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
231→
232→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
233→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
234→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
235→        } else {
236→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
237→        }
238→
239→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
240→        node: ASTNode,
241→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
242→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
243→    ) {
244→        node
245→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
246→            .nextCodeLeaf()
247→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
248→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
249→
250→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
251→                    emit(
252→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
253→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
254→                        true,
255→                    )
256→                    if (autoCorrect) {
257→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
258→                    }
259→                }
260→            }
261→    }
262→
263→    private fun visitAnnotation(
264→        node: ASTNode,
265→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
267→    ) {
268→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
269→
270→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
271→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
272→        ) {
273→            emit(
274→                node.startOffset,
275→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
276→                true,
277→            )
278→            if (autoCorrect) {
279→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
280→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
281→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
282→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
283→                }
284→            }
285→        }
286→    }
287→
288→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
289→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
290→        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first
291→        // text in the file
292→        val newLineWithIndent = nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text ?: "\n"
293→        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
294→            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
295→            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
296→        } else {
297→            newLineWithIndent
298→        }
299→    }
300→}
301→
302→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
303→
